acceptance_tests: expert_memo.md includes explicit citations to workbook cells/named ranges for all calculations referenced in the memo.
safety_PLC_1.xlsx contains SENSITIVITY sheet with parameter sweep and outputs consistent with memo claims.
CSV/JSON extract reproduces numeric tables and is consistent with workbook results.
Verifier can confirm workbook zip structure contains formula-bearing cells and named ranges via package-level inspection; when openpyxl is available, cell-level checks match the memo citations.
post_revision_steps: Run deterministic precheck to ensure artifacts present.
Run verifier checks and attach verifier notes to the attempt.
Include a one-line summary in final_message.txt highlighting the added sensitivity analysis and where to find cell-level citations.

planned_changes: Add a 'SENSITIVITY' worksheet to safety_PLC_1.xlsx that parameterizes the provisional threshold and remediation percent deltas. Implement a small sweep (suggested thresholds: 80, 90, 100, 110, 120; remediation percent perturbations: -10%, 0%, +10% around each stated delta) and compute adjusted values, pass/fail for each signal, combined pass, total_gap, and budget fit for every scenario.

Document remediation interpretation explicitly in the workbook and memo: show formula(s) for multiplicative application (value * (1 + delta)) and an alternative additive interpretation (value + delta_absolute) if relevant. Add a comparison table showing results under both interpretations.

Embed explicit cell/range citations into expert_memo.md (sheet!cell or named_range) for: raw input table, provisional_threshold cell, adjusted values block, total_gap formula, budget arithmetic, and the sensitivity table outputs.
Add named ranges to the workbook for key items (RAW_TABLE, PROVISIONAL_THRESHOLD, ADJUSTED_VALUES, TOTAL_GAP, OPTIONS_COSTS, BUDGET) and reference them in memo citations to make provenance clear.
Produce and include a machine-checkable extract [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] (or .json) that contains the same numeric tables as the memo; ensure build_safety_plc.py can regenerate this extract.
Update build_safety_plc.py to generate the sensitivity sheet and the CSV/JSON extract, and add a short README section in expert_memo.md describing how to run the script and verify the results.
Re-run package-level inspection and include a small verification note in final_message.txt that lists the named ranges and the presence of formula cells (so verifiers know what to check).
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Address limited sensitivity analysis and workbook-to-memo traceability so reviewers and automated verifiers can reproduce calculations and evaluate alternate remediation interpretations.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline deliverables meet artifact-contract requirements and correctly flag the missing truth-threshold policy; the memo includes provisional classification, budget arithmetic, and an explicit recommendation to hold. Shortfalls that prevented full confidence: (1) memo lacks explicit cell/range citations linking its calculations to the workbook formulas, (2) sensitivity analysis is minimal - only a single provisional threshold and one-point remediation adjustments are shown, and (3) no documented sensitivity for alternative interpretations of the percent-delta remediation (e.g., multiplicative vs additive) or local perturbation sweeps. Also note a tooling limitation: openpyxl preview was unavailable and package-level inspection was used instead; the attempt did create a build script (build_safety_plc.py) which aids reproducibility.
